name: Sitcoms
description: The situation-comedy genre — landmark American and British sitcoms, iconic characters, and catchphrases

questions.q: What does the word 'sitcom' stand for?

source.notes: The term dates to the early radio era.

questions.q: Which iconic 1994 NBC sitcom features six friends who hang out at the Central Perk coffee shop?

source.notes: Ran 1994-2004 on NBC.

questions.q: Who plays the food-loving actor Joey Tribbiani on Friends?

source.notes: Led the short-lived Friends spin-off Joey (2004-2006).

questions.q: Which Joey Tribbiani catchphrase has become a classic pick-up line?

source.notes: Often delivered with his eyebrows raised.

questions.q: Which NBC sitcom set in a Boston bar played under the tagline 'Where Everybody Knows Your Name'?

source.notes: Ran 1982-1993; spun off Frasier.

questions.q: Which sitcom follows Will Smith living with his wealthy aunt and uncle's family in Los Angeles?

source.notes: Ran 1990-1996 on NBC.

questions.q: Which genre-defining 1990s sitcom was famously 'a show about nothing' set in New York?

source.notes: Co-created by Jerry Seinfeld and Larry David.

questions.q: Which NBC mockumentary sitcom premiered in 2005 as the U.S. adaptation of a BBC hit?

source.notes: Developed by Greg Daniels.

questions.q: Which 2009 Amy Poehler NBC sitcom is set in the parks department of Pawnee, Indiana?

source.notes: Created by Greg Daniels and Michael Schur.

questions.q: Who performed 'I'll Be There for You,' the Friends theme song?

source.notes: Co-written with Friends co-creators Marta Kauffman and David Crane.

questions.q: 'Bazinga!' is the catchphrase of which character on The Big Bang Theory?

source.notes: Played by Jim Parsons; four-time Emmy winner for the role.

questions.q: Which 1970s ABC sitcom featured a blended family with three boys, three girls, and a housekeeper?

source.notes: Ran on ABC 1969-1974.

questions.q: Which 1985-1992 Miami-set NBC sitcom starred Bea Arthur, Betty White, Rue McClanahan, and Estelle Getty?

source.notes: Created by Susan Harris.

questions.q: Which 1980s sitcom featured Michael J. Fox as a young Republican growing up with former-hippie parents?

source.notes: Ran 1982-1989 on NBC.

questions.q: Which dysfunctional-family sitcom features the Bluths and a frozen banana stand?

source.notes: Created by Mitchell Hurwitz; narrated by Ron Howard.

questions.q: Which 1990s sitcom gave us Tim 'The Tool Man' Taylor and his show-within-a-show, Tool Time?

source.notes: Starred Tim Allen; ran 1991-1999 on ABC.

questions.q: Which 1989-1998 ABC family sitcom launched Steve Urkel's catchphrase 'Did I do that?'

source.notes: Urkel became the show's breakout character.

questions.q: Which 2005-2014 CBS sitcom revolved around architect Ted Mosby narrating to his future kids?

source.notes: Narrated by Bob Saget.

questions.q: Which Ricky Gervais mockumentary sitcom originated in the UK in 2001 and spawned international versions?

source.notes: BBC sitcom written by Gervais and Stephen Merchant.

questions.q: Which NBC sitcom spin-off of Cheers centered on a pompous psychiatrist hosting a Seattle radio show?

source.notes: Starred Kelsey Grammer; ran 1993-2004.

questions.q: Which 1951-1957 CBS sitcom starring Lucille Ball is widely considered the first major American sitcom hit?

source.notes: Pioneered the three-camera sitcom format.

questions.q: Which 2015-2020 CBC/Pop TV Canadian sitcom follows a wealthy family who loses everything and moves to a small town?

source.notes: Swept the 2020 Emmys comedy categories.
